title: peer-supplied receipt_type written to delivery_state without validation
description: A remote peer sends a ClearMessage::PeerReceipt with an arbitrary receipt_type string. dispatch_peer_receipt() in relay.rs passes that string directly to store.update_message_delivery_state() which writes it unchanged to the delivery_state TEXT column with no validation. While the peer is MLS-authenticated, they can write any string as delivery_state, corrupting delivery tracking for messages they did not send. Fix: enumerate valid delivery state strings (e.g. 'delivered', 'read') and reject unknown values before the DB write.
